children.name: Eulacestoma
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Wattled Ploughbill, a unique passerine endemic to the montane forests of New Guinea. Its most remarkable feature is the male's large, circular, fleshy pink wattle on the side of the face, which is expanded during display. The genus name refers to its plough-shaped bill, an adaptation for its specialised foraging technique.

children.name: Eulacestoma nigropectus
children.description: A medium-sized, olive-brown bird with a unique vertical pink wattle on the face of the male and a distinctive laterally compressed bill used to plough through dead leaves in search of insects, endemic to the highlands of New Guinea. It uses its specialised bill to pry open curled dead leaves, a foraging technique unlike any other passerine. It keeps to dense undergrowth in mid-montane forests and is more often heard than seen.
